2605.22645.title: AtelierEval: Agentic Evaluation of Humans & LLMs as Text-to-Image Prompters
2605.22645.paper_summary: Text-to-image (T2I) systems increasingly rely on upstream prompters, either humans or multimodal large language models (MLLMs), to translate user intent into detailed prompts. Yet current benchmarks fix the prompt and only evaluate T2I models, leaving the prompting proficiency of this upstream component entirely unmeasured. We introduce AtelierEval, the first unified benchmark that quantifies prompting proficiency across 360 expert-crafted tasks. Grounded in a cognitive view, it spans three task categories and instantiates tasks using a taxonomy of real-world challenges, with a dual interface for both humans and MLLMs. To enable scalable and reliable evaluation, we propose AtelierJudge, a skill-based, memory-augmented agentic evaluator. It produces subjective and objective scores for prompt-image pairs, achieving a Spearman correlation of 0.79 with human experts, approaching human performance. Extensive experiments benchmark 8 MLLMs against 48 human users across 4 T2I backends, validate AtelierEval as a robust diagnostic tool, and reveal the superiority of mimicry over planning, advocating for an image-augmented direction for future prompters. Our work is released to support future research.
2605.22645.paper_summary_zh: 文本到圖像（T2I）系統越來越依賴上游提示者，無論是人類還是多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs），將用戶意圖轉化為詳細的提示。<lb/>然而，目前的基準固定了提示，只評估T2I模型，完全未測量這一上游組件的提示能力。<lb/>我們介紹了AtelierEval，第一個統一基準，量化360個專家精心設計的任務中的提示能力。<lb/>基於認知觀點，它涵蓋三個任務類別，並使用現實世界挑戰的分類法來實現任務，為人類和MLLMs提供雙重界面。<lb/>為了實現可擴展和可靠的評估，我們提出了AtelierJudge，一個基於技能的、增強記憶的主動評估者。<lb/>它為提示-圖像對生成主觀和客觀分數，與人類專家的斯皮爾曼相關係數達到0.79，接近人類表現。<lb/>大量實驗將8個MLLMs與48名人類用戶在4個T2I後端進行基準測試，驗證了AtelierEval作為一個強健的診斷工具，並揭示了模仿優於規劃的優越性，倡導未來提示者朝向圖像增強的方向發展。<lb/>我們的工作已發布以支持未來的研究。
2605.22645.author: Hanjun Luo et.al.
2605.22645.authors: Hanjun Luo, Zhimu Huang, Sylvia Chung, Yiran Wang, Yingbin Jin, Jialin Li, Jiang Li, Xinfeng Li, Hanan Salam
